mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 1.5% signal monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as rates rise in the tightening cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.21% as of 2022-06-15, with Fed in active tightening mode
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2022-06-15, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE > 4.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand that foreshadows earnings misses and inventory corrections, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months following RSAFS declines > 1.5%, or Rank IC between RSAFS changes and forward returns is < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 8.0%, signaling stagflationary conditions where supply constraints persist despite weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as margin compression accelerates.
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with persistent supply shocks
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods meeting both trigger conditions, or the joint effect of INDPRO deceleration and elevated CPIAUCSL does not produce significantly lower returns than either signal alone (interaction F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units annualized while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month, signaling synchronized contraction in construction and labor demand that foreshadows broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1400 thousand AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and labor sectors
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both HOUST < 1400 and PAYEMS growth < 0.1%, or the interaction term between HOUST and PAYEMS changes explains less than 5% additional variance beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month signals robust labor market tightness that sustains consumer spending and corporate hiring momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as near-term earnings confidence strengthens.
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong hiring momentum
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months following PAYEMS growth > 0.3%, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 8.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling the beginning of a monetary easing cycle where real rates compress and equity risk premiums contract, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as rate-cut expectations build.
regime_hint: early-stage disinflation with accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL < 8.0% and FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or the joint signal produces lower returns than FEDFUNDS alone in more than 60% of test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) growth turns negative, signaling recession-like demand contraction that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings estimates are revised downward across sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS growth < 0% YoY
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth explains less than 8% additional variance beyond univariate models over any 3-year rolling window.
